_source: Mythras Core Rulebook (3rd Printing, 2018), pp.251-252, adapted for Gloranthan mounts
_notes: Horse stats are directly from Mythras Core p.252. Other mounts derived from RQG Starter Set pregen folios, adapted to Mythras mechanics. War variants add +4 STR and +4 SIZ to base stats.

horse.name: Horse (Riding)
horse.description: Standard riding mount available to most adventurers. For a War Horse, add +4 to STR and SIZ.
horse.skills: Athletics 52%, Brawn 83%, Endurance 66%, Evade 40%, Perception 48%, Willpower 34%
horse.notes: A riding horse will only attack once per melee round on its own, or its owner may attack. Normally, a riding horse will only attack if its life is threatened.

warHorse.name: Horse (War-trained)
warHorse.description: Specially trained to fight against foot troops. Requires a competent rider to initiate battle.
warHorse.skills: Athletics 52%, Brawn 91%, Endurance 66%, Evade 40%, Perception 48%, Willpower 34%
warHorse.notes: War horses attack on their own initiative. Can bite and kick one or two foes, or rear and plunge against one foe.

bison.name: Bison (War-trained cavalry)
bison.description: Praxian war mount. Larger and stronger than a horse but less agile. Used by the Bison Rider tribe.
bison.skills: Athletics 42%, Brawn 90%, Endurance 72%, Evade 30%, Perception 44%, Willpower 38%
bison.notes: A bison can head butt or trample in the same melee round, but not both. When a rider uses their lance while charging, use the bison's damage modifier (+2d6) instead of the rider's.

highLlama.name: High Llama (War-trained)
highLlama.description: Praxian war mount. Tall and fast, used by the High Llama Rider tribe. Larger than a horse.
highLlama.skills: Athletics 40%, Brawn 86%, Endurance 66%, Evade 28%, Perception 44%, Willpower 38%
highLlama.notes: A high llama can bite and kick one or two foes at the same time, or can rear and plunge against one foe. Due to its height, riders are difficult to reach from the ground (-1 grade to melee attacks from below).

sableAntelope.name: Sable Antelope (War-trained)
sableAntelope.description: Praxian war mount. Smaller than a horse but very agile. Used by the Sable Rider tribe.
sableAntelope.skills: Athletics 56%, Brawn 65%, Endurance 62%, Evade 52%, Perception 48%, Willpower 42%
sableAntelope.notes: A sable antelope will either butt or bite and kick in a melee round. Fast and agile, better at evasion than raw power.

shadowcat.name: Shadowcat
shadowcat.description: A large wildcat sacred to Yinkin, brother of Orlanth. Intelligent enough to form bonds with humans. Size of a lynx or small panther.
shadowcat.skills: Athletics 70%, Brawn 43%, Endurance 50%, Evade 74%, Perception 66%, Stealth 80%, Survival 60%, Track 55%, Willpower 50%
shadowcat.magic: None (though bonded shadowcats share Spirit Magic with their companion)
shadowcat.notes: First attacks with both claws and bite simultaneously. If both claws hit, Rurik hangs on and rips with its hind claws on the next round while continuing to bite. Sacred to Yinkin; hunters and Odayla worshippers sometimes bond with them.

baboon.name: Baboon (Companion)
baboon.description: Intelligent Gloranthan baboons are fully sapient. They can be companions, allies, or even cult members. Cousin Monkey-type baboons are smaller and less combat-focused.
baboon.skills: Athletics 57%, Brawn 43%, Climb 90%, Endurance 50%, Evade 56%, Perception 54%, Stealth 46%, Willpower 52%
baboon.magic: Spirit Magic (varies): Countermagic 2, Heal 2, Mobility 1, Spirit Screen 2
baboon.notes: Gloranthan baboons are sapient. They can speak (poorly) in human tongues and practice spirit magic. They worship ancestor spirits and can serve as companions to shamans and Waha worshippers.

_companionRules.source: Mythras Core p.251+ (Creatures chapter), adapted for AiG character creation
whoGetsCompanions: Praxian culture characters: All Praxian warriors and nomads have a tribal mount (Bison, High Llama, Sable, Impala, or Rhino depending on tribe)
Characters with Ride skill and appropriate social class: May own a riding horse or war horse
Shamans (Waha cult): Often have spirit-bonded animal companions
Odayla worshippers: May bond with shadowcats or other forest creatures
Grazelanders: Horse-riding culture, all get riding horses

attributeCalculation.hitPoints: CON + SIZ, distributed per hit location table (same as player characters)
attributeCalculation.damageModifier: STR + SIZ lookup on standard table
attributeCalculation.strikeRank: DEX + INS / 2 (or DEX + INT for sapients)

combatNotes.mountedCombat: When charging with a lance, use the mount's damage modifier instead of the rider's
combatNotes.hitLocationRoll: Attacks vs mounted rider: 1d10+10 targets rider; unmodified d20 targets mount
combatNotes.mountActions: Mounts act on their own initiative unless directly controlled by rider (requires Ride skill roll)
